task_context.description: This is a systematic electromagnetic calorimeter performance study that investigates the relationship between detector depth and shower containment efficiency. The task involves conducting Monte Carlo simulations using Geant4 to model electromagnetic showers produced by electrons in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) calorimeter. The study requires testing three different calorimeter depths (16, 20, and 25 radiation lengths) across multiple beam energies to quantify energy containment, resolution, and shower development characteristics. The task combines detector physics simulation with performance optimization analysis to determine minimum detector thickness requirements for acceptable energy leakage levels.
task_context.goal: Determine the minimum calorimeter depth required to keep energy leakage below 1% at 20 GeV and characterize how calorimeter depth affects electromagnetic shower containment across different energies
constraints: Energy leakage must be below 1% at 20 GeV
Use homogeneous PbWO4 calorimeter material only
Limited to 3 energy points for tractable simulation
Run one depth configuration per geometry call

{'report': '# Electromagnetic Calorimeter Performance Study - Execution Report

## Executive Summary

This study investigated the relationship between detector depth and shower containment efficiency in a homogeneous lead tungstate (PbWO4) electromagnetic calorimeter using Monte Carlo simulations.
